title: LitT encoder determinism — pin the literal-table bytes for cross-consumer identity
purpose: The `LitT` encoder compresses the literal-table body with `Compression::default()` over a caret-versioned `flate2 = "1.1.9"` (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:43`; `crates/beamr/Cargo.toml:26`, both at `d60f826`). Compressed output is not a stable function of input: it varies with the flate2/miniz_oxide version and backend that the FINAL build graph resolves, so identical literal tables can encode to different bytes on different builds with zero code changes on beamr's side. CLAIM SCOPE, ruled by the claim-holder (Vesper Lynd via the board, 2026-07-15, Pole-B-critical): CROSS-CONSUMER determinism — aion hashes emitted BEAM bytes into durable package version identity, so identical literal tables must encode identically for downstreams resolving their own dependency graphs. A lockfile-plus-golden-fixture defence is insufficient for that claim: the fixture runs in beamr's CI, not the consumer's build, so a downstream with a different miniz_oxide drifts silently past the wall. The decode side is indifferent to the fix: `decode_literal_chunk` accepts a zero size-prefix as an UNCOMPRESSED table read directly, and otherwise inflates bounded (`crates/beamr/src/loader/decode/chunks.rs:307-313`; bomb guard `crates/beamr/src/loader/decode/chunks.rs:350-374`). MEASURED GROUND at `d60f826`: all 22 committed `.beam` fixtures that carry a `LitT` chunk use the zero-prefix UNCOMPRESSED form — the erlc/gleam toolchain that produced the committed corpus does not compress literal tables. Re-deflating those 22 raw tables at level 6 saves 2,448 bytes total across the corpus (43% of 5,657 raw table bytes; the largest single table is 1,830 bytes raw → 746 deflated). Compression on these tables buys kilobytes per corpus, not per module.
task: Make `encode_literal_chunk` emit bytes that are a pure function of the literal table — no ambient compressor default, no dependency-resolved variance — and wall the result with a committed golden fixture. `Compression::default()` dies in this file. THREE CANDIDATES, decision at the tear: (A) EXACT-PIN the backend — `=`-pin flate2 and add a direct `=`-pinned miniz_oxide dependency so every consumer graph unifies on those exact bytes. Delivers the claim but exports resolution rigidity onto every downstream sharing miniz_oxide, and re-fragilizes at every flate2 minimum-version bump; the golden fixture then guards a dependency's mood, not a construction. (B) DETERMINISM BY CONSTRUCTION, stored-block zlib — beamr emits its own canonical stored-block zlib stream (fixed segmentation at the 65,535-byte block bound, adler32 trailer; a small emitter producing valid zlib for any inflater). Bytes independent of any compressor's choices forever; measured cost ~11 bytes of framing per table versus the raw form, i.e. the full deflate saving (43% of table bytes on the corpus) is forgone. The golden fixture then guards construction. (C) RECOMMENDED — emit the UNCOMPRESSED zero-prefix form the decoder already accepts and the committed toolchain corpus already uses: size prefix 0, raw table bytes. Determinism by construction with NO compressor and NO new emitter — the change is a deletion; flate2 leaves the encode path entirely (decode keeps it for legacy compressed chunks); beamr-emitted modules become byte-symmetric with what erlc/gleam themselves emit in the committed corpus (all 22 LitT fixtures, verifiable with `docs/design/beamr/briefs/`-independent parsing of `crates/beamr/tests/fixtures/**.beam`). Size cost identical to B minus the zlib framing. RESIDUAL RISK, named: a foreign (non-beamr) consumer of beamr-EMITTED `.beam` files whose loader predates zero-prefix support would fail to load them; beamr's own decoder accepts the form at the cited bytes and the current toolchain emits it, but the brief makes no claim about older third-party loaders. DRAFTER RECOMMENDATION: C, with B as the fallback if the tear rules that beamr-emitted `.beam` must carry zlib-framed literal tables for foreign-loader compatibility. OPEN QUESTIONS FOR THE TEAR: (1) candidate selection A/B/C; (2) whether beamr-emitted `.beam` must remain loadable by third-party OTP tooling older than zero-prefix support — if yes, C is dead and B is the shape; (3) golden-fixture placement (committed binary fixture versus in-test expected byte array). Any need to change the decode side, any new configuration surface, or any silent fallback to an ambient compressor default is STOP-and-report.

requirements.title: Replace the ambient-default compression call with the tear-ruled deterministic form
requirements.spec: Rework the tail of `encode_literal_chunk` (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:42-54` at `d60f826`) per the ruled candidate. Under C: write a zero u32 size prefix followed by the raw payload — the exact form `decode_literal_chunk` reads at `crates/beamr/src/loader/decode/chunks.rs:308-310` and the form every committed toolchain fixture carries; remove the `flate2` imports from this file (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:13-14`) and retire `EncodeError::CompressionFailed` if nothing else raises it (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/container.rs:34-45`). Under B: emit a canonical stored-block zlib stream — fixed 2-byte header, stored blocks segmented at the 65,535-byte bound with the standard 5-byte block headers, adler32 trailer — behind a named constant and a rationale comment pinning the exact framing; no flate2 involvement on the encode path. Under A: `=`-pin `flate2` and add a direct `=`-pinned `miniz_oxide` dependency with a rationale comment naming the determinism claim; `Compression::default()` still dies in favour of a named level constant. In every candidate: the encoder consults no ambient default, no environment, and no configuration surface; the emitted bytes are a pure function of the literal table. The module documentation comment (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:1-11`) is updated in the same commit to describe the ruled form — a stale doc claiming zlib compression over a zero-prefix emitter is a truthfulness defect.
acceptance: Encoding the same literal table twice yields byte-identical output, and `Compression::default()` appears nowhere under `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/`

Under C: a fixture-shaped module encoded by beamr carries a `LitT` chunk whose first four bytes are zero and whose remainder is the raw table, and `decode_literal_chunk` round-trips it; under B: the emitted stream inflates correctly through the existing bounded decode path; under A: the pinned versions are exact (`=`) and named in a rationale comment
The existing loader round-trip and encode tests remain green unmodified except where they pin the old compressed form
The module doc comment describes the ruled form accurately

requirements.title: Wall the bytes with a committed golden fixture covering every Literal variant
requirements.spec: Add a golden-bytes wall: encode one representative literal table exercising every `Literal` variant and every width boundary the encoder branches on — `SMALL_INTEGER_EXT`/`INTEGER_EXT`/`SMALL_BIG_EXT` integer widths (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:125-143`), float, untrimmed big-integer magnitude (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:149-166`), small and large atom names, binary, small and large tuple, nil, list with non-nil tail, map, string, and export fun — and assert byte-exact equality against committed golden bytes (placement per tear: committed binary fixture or in-test expected array). Assert the golden bytes also DECODE back to the identical literal table through `decode_literal_chunk`, so the wall pins the round trip, not just the emitter. Proposed names `litt_encode_matches_golden_bytes` and `litt_golden_bytes_round_trip` (the tear may rename; any rename updates this brief's mapping in the same commit). This wall is what fails loudly if any future change — dependency, emitter, or refactor — moves the emitted bytes: under B/C it guards construction; under A it is the tripwire for a dependency drift that the pin was supposed to prevent.
acceptance: `litt_encode_matches_golden_bytes` asserts byte-exact equality between a fresh encode of the representative table and the committed golden bytes, and covers every `Literal` variant and integer-width branch
`litt_golden_bytes_round_trip` decodes the committed golden bytes and asserts structural equality with the source table
Fail-first, recorded and reverted: perturb one emitted byte (or, under A, bump the pinned level) and show the golden wall fails
Both walls pass ten consecutive isolated runs

requirements.title: State the identity shift out loud — changed bytes move downstream version hashes
requirements.spec: The claim-holder's reviewer note, carried as a requirement so it is stated rather than discovered (Vesper Lynd, 2026-07-15): pinning changes the encoded bytes versus today's default-deflate output, which shifts aion package version hashes on the next recompile of any direct-BEAM package — EXPECTED AND CORRECT (version identity moves when bytes move), and never to be read as a regression. Add a CHANGELOG entry under the next unreleased version stating: the `LitT` chunk form changed (naming the ruled form), emitted `.beam` bytes differ from 0.15.2's output for any module with literals, downstream content hashes over emitted bytes will shift once, and the decode side is unchanged and loads both forms. The build handoff carries the same statement plus the claim-scope line (cross-consumer determinism, aion version identity). Version-number roll itself stays with the release process (`scripts/release.sh`, project-lead gated) and is out of scope here.
acceptance: CHANGELOG.md carries the identity-shift entry with the ruled form named and the one-time hash-shift stated as expected behaviour
The handoff records the claim scope and the hash-shift statement
No version-number change is made in this brief

boundaries: CLAIM CARVE-OUT, recorded verbatim from the claim-holder (Vesper Lynd via Waffles, 2026-07-15): (1) NO COLLISION — nothing in-flight on the aion/AWL side edits literals.rs or changes encode output bytes; loader/encode is consumed as an API only. (2) CARVE-OUT CONSENTED as scoped: the compression call plus the golden fixture, NOTHING ELSE in `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` is touchable under this brief, and Vesper Lynd is MANDATORY REVIEWER on the final diff. (3) CLAIM SCOPE: cross-consumer determinism — aion hashes emitted BEAM bytes into durable package version identity, so lockfile-only protection does not protect the actual consumer.

Decode side untouched: `decode_literal_chunk` continues to accept BOTH the zero-prefix uncompressed form and the bounded-inflate compressed form (`crates/beamr/src/loader/decode/chunks.rs:307-313`) — legacy `.beam` files must keep loading forever; flate2 remains a dependency for decode regardless of the ruled candidate.
No new configuration surface, no environment consultation, no silent fallback: the emitted bytes are a pure function of the literal table, or the encoder refuses loudly.
No dependency additions except candidate A's `=`-pinned miniz_oxide, if A is ruled.

Laws stated where they bind: believed-state-is-not-citable-state — every current-state claim above is pinned to file:line at `d60f826`, re-verified at the bytes, never to a research artifact or board message; the measured corpus numbers are reproducible from `crates/beamr/tests/fixtures/**.beam` with a container parse.
Untouchable beyond the carve-out: the rest of `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` and branches `awl-bc1-*` (Vesper's claim).
verification: Run the house bar on the final head: `cargo fmt --all --check`; touch crate roots, then `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and fresh `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`; `cargo test -p beamr --lib` (default and `--all-features`; telemetry::lifecycle has a known parallel-run flake — rerun standalone and report both honestly if it fires); `cargo test --workspace --all-features --test '*'`; the edge-worker npm leg; both locked wasm32 check commands and the pinned wasm test command with its exact-name inventory unchanged (this brief adds no wasm-inventory names). Run both golden walls in isolation ten consecutive times. Perform, record, and revert R2's fail-first perturbation. Stamp the handoff with final `git rev-parse HEAD`, `rustc --version --verbose` (first two lines), `cargo --version`, `node --version`, UTC timestamps, exact commands, the ruled candidate and its implementation point, the golden fixture location, the identity-shift statement, and the claim-scope line. Vesper Lynd's mandatory review on the final diff happens before landing; the tear's reviewer-of-record process governs as usual.
